look.text: Counter, fridge, cabinets of cereal you didn't buy and won't replace. A whole kitchen, and not one thing in it is yours. Classic.
The big window, and the same grey city through it, now with actual depth behind the glass. The building across the way judges you in three dimensions.
The green couch. THE couch. It sits back where the perspective says it should, and you shrink a little as you approach — the depth band, doing its quiet job.

use.text: You sink into the rendered couch. Schematically, it is perfect. Spiritually, you miss the hand-drawn one, and that's the whole show.

look.text: A green bike, hung on the wall, ridden never. It is decor. It is a lifestyle you are only pretending to have. You respect it enormously.

exits.name: the door
exits.look: Through the rendered door — and straight into the hand-drawn version of the same room. Mind the dimensional seam.
